text.title: PR Time + Cost Diff Tool Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules pinning when the checker fires, what it reports, and the skip path.

rule.statement: Checker MUST trigger only on PRs that touch at least one of: prompts dir, model id config, context-assembly module, token-budget config. PRs that touch only docs / tests are skipped.
rule.rationale: Replaying eval on every PR is too expensive; surface-trigger keeps CI minutes within budget.

rule.statement: Eval replay MUST use a fixed seed (temperature 0 OR fixed sampler seed) AND a frozen eval set hash. Replay variance is forbidden as a source of "diff" signal.
rule.rationale: Non-deterministic replay produces noise reviewers ignore; determinism is what makes the diff trustworthy.

rule.statement: Report MUST include three deltas vs main: median latency Δms, p95 latency Δms, $/req Δcents (or Δusd if rate > 1¢). Aggregate-only reports are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Median hides p95 spikes; cost-only hides latency regressions. Three deltas are the minimum.

rule.statement: Report MUST also include eval pass-rate delta (Δaccuracy / ΔBLEU / Δtask-specific metric). A PR that "saves cost" but tanks accuracy MUST surface that loss.
rule.rationale: Cost cuts without accuracy tracking ship silent regressions.

rule.statement: Cost table MUST be versioned in repo (e.g., `cost-table.yaml`) and pinned per PR run. Vendor price drift between runs is recorded as a separate "table updated" PR, not blended into cost diffs.
rule.rationale: Without versioning, every vendor price tweak shows as a code-change cost delta.

rule.statement: If $/req delta > published per-PR budget (default +20%) OR p95 latency delta > +30%, checker MUST fail the required check; merge is blocked until override or change.
rule.rationale: Without a blocking budget, "cost-positive" PRs accumulate.

rule.statement: If repo has no LLM pipeline OR no eval set OR pipeline is non-deterministic without a seed, STOP.
rule.rationale: Skip applies when replay produces noise, not signal.
